Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 247 [Dream rituals][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

* NA DIBti DINGIR u d15 UGU-šu₂ GAL₂ši

(o 1) (If) a man, the wrath of god and goddess is against him;

o 22

E₂.⸢GAL⸣-šu₂ u LUGAL-šu₂ la ma-gir-šu₂

(o 2) his palace and his king are unfavourable towards him, (but) slander him (and) and gossip about him;

o 33

kar-⸢ṣi⸣-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ dib-bi-šu₂ i-dab-bu-bu

o 44

ta-di-⸢ra⸣-ti ul-ta-⸢dir₂⸣

(o 4) he is made gloomy by fits of depression;

o 55

ki-la ⸢u₃!(DI.LU) me-se-ra IGI.IGImar

(o 5) he constantly experiences confinement and hardship;

o 66

ina ⸢E₂ DU₁₄⸣ ina SILA pu-uḫ-pu-ḫu-u₂ GAR-šu₂

(o 6) in the house there is combat, on the street strife is placed for him;

o 77

ŠU.⸢SI ḪUL⸣ti EGIR-šu₂ tar-⸢ṣa⸣-at

(o 7) an evil finger is pointed behind him;

o 88

MAŠ₂.GE₆-MEŠ ⸢par₂⸣-da-a-ti IGI.IGImar

(o 8) he constantly sees frightening dreams;

o 99

INIM.GAR-šu₂ ⸢ḪUL? da⸣-bi-ib KI-šu₂

(o 9) his reputation is bad; the interlocutor never speaks the truth with him; he displeases the one who sees him.

o 1010

kit-ta la ⸢i-da⸣-bu-ub UGU IGI.[DU₈.A]-šu₂ GIG

o 1111

a-na UGU [...] x-nu-šu₂

(o 11) On [...] ....

o 1212

⸢E₂⸣.GAL-⸢šu₂ u₃⸣ LUGAL-šu₂

(o 12) To satisfy and reconcile his palace and his king;

o 1313

a-na DU₁₀.GA ⸢u₃⸣ SILIM.Ami

o 1414

ḪUL-šu₂ ⸢a-na dup-pu⸣-ri₃

(o 14) to withdraw his evil.

o 1515

[DU₃.DU₃]-BI u₂tar-muš u₂IGI-lim

(o 15) Its [ritual]: you put lupin, imhur-līm-plant, imhur-ešrā-plant, the lower part of a grindstone (and) kamkadu-plant [in a leather (bag)] around his neck;

o 1616

[u₂]⸢IGI⸣.20 um-mat na₄UR₅ u₂kam-ka-du

o 1717

[ina KUŠ] ina GU₂-šu₂ GARan

o 1818

[...] x-šu₂ i-⸢la?⸣-ʾu-ut

(o 18) he swallows his [...] ...;

o 1919

[...] NIG₂.NA ⸢šimLI⸣ GARan

(o 19) [...] you set up a censer of burāšu-juniper;

o 2020

[...] ⸢BAL⸣qi₂ ⸢en₂? ḫe₂?⸣-silim-ma-ŋu₁₀

(o 20) you libate [...]. Incantation: may he be reconciled with me!

Reverse
r 1r 1

[en₂] diŋir-ŋu₁₀ ⸢ḫe₂-silim⸣-ma-⸢ŋu₁₀⸣

(r 1) [Incantation:] May my god be reconciled with me!

r 22

⸢ama⸣-dinana-ŋu₁₀ ⸢ḫe₂⸣-silim-ma-ŋu₁₀

(r 2) May my goddess be reconciled with me!

r 33

a₂ zid-da diŋir-ŋu₁₀ ⸢ḫe₂⸣-silim-ma-ŋu₁₀

(r 3) May my god be reconciled1 with me on the right!

r 44

igi-ŋu₁₀ ama-dinana-⸢ŋu₁₀⸣ ḫe₂-gub

(r 4) May my goddess stand before me!

r 55

igi!(BAR) dnin-urta lugal ŋeš⸢tukul⸣-ke₄ ḫe₂-gub

(r 5) May (my god) stand before Ninurta, king of the weapon!

r 66

inim-inim nu-dug₃-ga-ŋu₁₀ ⸢inim⸣ nu-silim-ma

(r 6) Unfavourable words for me, word(s) of non-reconciliation.

r 77

a-⸢na⸣ ia-a-ši ⸢re⸣-ṣa-⸢nim⸣-ma

(r 7) Help me and I will sing your praise! Incantation formula.

r 88

⸢da₃⸣-li₂-li₂-ku-nu ⸢lud⸣-lul ⸢TU₆⸣ EN₂

r 99

⸢EN₂⸣ a-na DINGIR-ia₅ ⸢mi⸣-na-a e-pu-⸢uš⸣

(r 9) Incantation: What did I do to my god? What did I do wrong towards my goddess that my god was angry with me (and) my goddess was displeased with me?

r 1010

a-na d⸢iš⸣-tar-⸢ia₅ mi-na⸣-a aḫ-[ṭi]

r 1111

DINGIR-MU iz-nu-⸢u₂⸣ KI-ia

r 1212

diš-tar-i₁₄ ⸢is⸣-bu-su UGU-ia

r 1313

GIG lib₃-bi-⸢ia⸣ lim-da-ma

(r 13) Learn of my heartache and I will sing your praise! Incantation formula.

r 1414

da₃-li₂-li₂-ku-⸢nu lud⸣-lul TU₆ EN₂

r 1515

an-nam ⸢3⸣-šu₂ DU₁₁.GA-ma

(r 15) You say this (incantation) 3 times, you lie down, and you will see a favourable dream.

r 1616

NU₂-ma MAŠ₂.GE₆ SIG₅ IGImar

(colophon)
r 1717

GIM SUMUN-BI ša₃-ṭir-ma u ba-ri₃

(r 17) Written and checked according to its original. Writer: Nabu-ahu-iddina, scribal apprentice.

r 1818

ša₃-ṭir₂! md!MUATI—PAB—AŠ lu₂ŠAB.TUR

r 1919

ša₂ ina šur-qu i-šar-ri-qu ina dan!(E₂-)na-an

(r 19) Whoever steals (this tablet) by theft, removes (it) by force, (or) requests it (and) lets it be forgotten, may Nabu decree his annihilation!

r 2020

⸢e⸣-kim e-ri-ši u₂-šam-šu-u

r 2121

⸢d⸣MUATI ZAḪ₂-šu₂ ⸢liq⸣-bi

1 he₂-silim-ma-ŋu₁₀ is an error for he₂-gub , as gods stand (gub ) at a person's side (cf . r . 4 ; Butler 1998 : 311 , note to line 23)
